revision.text:
[[ja:一之御前神社]] {{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] |Name = '''Ichinomiya Gozen Shrine''' (一之御前神社) |image = [[File:Ichinomisaki-jinja (Atsuta-jingū) 2014.jpg|250px]]
Ichinomiya Gozen Shrine Head (一之御前神社社頭) |Location = {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}} {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|qid=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}} {{ill|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|en|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|qid=Q1355625|lt=Atsuta Ward|1=Atsuta-ku, Nagoya}} {{ill|Shingu (Nagoya)|ja|神宮 (名古屋市)|lt=Shingu|lt_ja=神宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shingu (Nagoya)|1=Jingū|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q30934394}} 1-1-1 (1丁目1番1号) |LatitudeDegrees = 35 |LatitudeMinutes = 7 |LatitudeSeconds = 40.63 |LatitudeIndicator = N (北緯) |LongitudeDegrees = 136 |LongitudeMinutes = 54 |LongitudeSeconds = 29.85 |LongitudeIndicator = E (東経) |MapCountryCode = JP-23 |Deities = {{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}} {{ill|Aramitama|ja|荒魂|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramitama|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 荒魂・和魂|1=Mitama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q495568}}"Atsuta Shrine": page 20 |SacredObject = |ShrineRank = |Founded = During the era of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Keikō Emperor|1=Emperor Keikō}} or during the reign of {{ill|Tenmu Emperor|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu Emperor|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} (various theories) |MainHall = |AlternateName = |OtherSites = |AnnualFestival = |Rituals = |Map = Nagoya }} {{Coordinates}} '''Ichinomiya Gozen Shrine''' (一之御前神社) is a shrine located in {{ill|Aichi Prefecture|en|Aichi Prefecture|qid=Q80434|lt=Aichi Prefecture|1=Aichi Prefecture}}, {{ill|Nagoya|en|Nagoya|qid=Q11751|lt=Nagoya|1=Nagoya}}, {{ill|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|en|Atsuta-ku, Nagoya|qid=Q1355625|lt=Atsuta Ward|1=Atsuta-ku, Nagoya}}. It is one of the {{ill|Keidai-Sessha|ja|Keidai-Sessha|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Keidai-Sessha|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} of {{ill|Atsuta Shrine|en|Atsuta Shrine|qid=Q482065|lt=Atsuta Shrine|1=Atsuta Shrine}}. ==Overview== The founding is said to have occurred either during the era of {{ill|Emperor Keikō|en|Emperor Keikō|qid=Q329723|lt=Keikō Emperor|1=Emperor Keikō}} or during the reign of {{ill|Tenmu Emperor|ja|Emperor Tenmu|1=Emperor Tenmu|2=ja|3=天武天皇|1=Emperor Tenmu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenmu Emperor|13=User:Immanuelle/Emperor Tenmu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q335181}} {{#tag:ref|"‘The palace of the Sun (Emperor Keikō) and the establishment of a separately enshrined Aramitama deity’ (from "Atsuta Shrine Historical Records: Compilation of Origins": page 139)"|group="Note"}}. It is located to the northwest of the main shrine, at the far end of the pilgrimage path known as "Kokoro no Komichi", and was relocated to its present site in {{ill|1893|en|1893|qid=Q7840|lt=1893|1=1893}} (Meiji 26); prior to that, it stood near the southeast of the current main shrine {{#tag:ref|"From the perspective of the former main hall, the direction was still to the northwest. (From "April 1893 Atsuta Shrine Reconstruction and Pre-Meiji Structures Location Estimation Diagram": "Nagoya City History Map")"|group="Note"}}. It enshrines '''Amaterasu Omikami Aramitama''' (あまてらすおおかみあらみたま) as its deity. The term “Aramitama” refers to a divine spirit that generates fierce, dynamic phenomena and is seen as a symbol of valor and initiative. The reason for its enshrinement at Atsuta Shrine is understood to be equivalent to the existence of {{ill|Ise Shrine|en|Ise Shrine|qid=Q687168|lt=Ise Grand Shrine|1=Ise Grand Shrine}}’s {{ill|Aramatsuri-no-Miya|ja|荒祭宮|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Aramatsuri Shrine|qq=}} ({{Coord|34|27|22|N|136|43|30|E|scale:2500}}). Referred to as "‘The First Attendant Deity of the Imperial Court’" {{#tag:ref|"From 'Atsuta Shrine Official Enshrinement Record' and 'Atsuta Shrine Record' "Atsuta Shrine Historical Records: Compilation of Origins": page 363, etc."|group="Note"}}, the shrine not only shares an extremely close relationship with the main shrine but also gives the impression of a loyal, trusted aide. During the main shrine’s rituals, it was long customary to first present offerings here—a practice said to carry the meaning of "oni-gui" (poison tasting) {{#tag:ref|"‘When enshrining the main shrine, offerings are first prepared at Aramisaki, as is commonly said to be 'oni-gui’" (from "Oshu Shrine Secret Tradition": "Atsuta Shrine Historical Records: Continued Compilation of Origins (Part I)": page 7)"|group="Note"}}.
From this, an alternative theory has long existed proposing that, either as a different deity or as a supplementary spirit, {{ill|Ootomo Takehito no Mikoto|ja|大伴武日命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ootomo Takehito no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} was enshrined. While {{ill|Amano Nobukage|ja|天野信景|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Amano Nobukage|1=Amano Sadakage|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6535030}} and {{ill|Fukada Masakazu|ja|深田正韶|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Fukada Masakazu|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} support this theory, {{ill|Tsuda Masao|ja|津田正生|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tsuda Masao|1=Tsuda Masanari|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q22123474}} dismisses it as "inconceivable""Owari Province Place Name Studies": page 75. According to the "Owari Chronicle", Ootomo Takehito no Mikoto was a warrior who, during Yamato Takeru’s eastern expedition, served as a deputy general on both flanks alongside {{ill|Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi no Takehiko|lt=Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja|吉備武彦命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吉備武彦}} (きびたけひこのみこと) {{#tag:ref|"From the Nihon Shoki (Volume 7, Emperor Keikō)""Kazana Nihon Shoki, Volume 1": page 289|group="Note"}}, and it is said that the placement of this shrine and {{ill|Ryujinja|ja|龍神社 (熱田神宮)|lt=Ryujinja|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ryujinja|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579187|1=Ryujinja}} (whose enshrined deity is Kibi Takehiko no Mikoto) flanking the main shrine like guards originates from this account"Owari Chronicle Atsuta": page 9.
Before the Edo period, it appears that Ryujinja and this shrine were regarded as a combined entity, and a petition requesting that they be recognized as separate shrines was submitted to the chief priest (dated June 5, 1686 ({{ill|Jōkyō 3|ja|貞享3|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Jōkyō 3|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid|qid=Q135579188|1=1686}} April 15))"Atsuta Shrine Historical Records: Construction and Relocation, Volume I": page 412{{#tag:ref|"Request stating 'Separate shrines for Ichinomiya and Ryujinja'."|group="Note"}}. This request was granted, and since then, Ichinomiya Gozen Shrine and Ryujinja have existed independently. As for the origin of the shrine’s name, works such as {{ill|Wahimeimyojoseki|ja|倭姫命世記|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Wahimeimyojoseki|1=Yamatohime no Mikoto Seiki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q131331326}} note that the character "魂" can be read as "Misaki", giving rise to the theory that it was written as "Ichinomisaki" (一ノ魂) {{#tag:ref|"From 'Atsuta Main and Subsidiary Shrine Divine Body Sacred Decree Collection Theory'""Atsuta Shrine Historical Records: Continued Compilation of Origins (Part I)": page 34|group="Note"}}. Another theory holds that the temporary shrine—where the {{ill|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|en|Kusanagi no Tsurugi|qid=Q14944|lt=Grass-cutting Sword|1=Kusanagi no Tsurugi}} (also known as the Kusanagi Sword), which was returned to the court in {{ill|686|en|686|qid=Q30619|lt=686|1=686}} (the first year of {{ill|Shuchō|en|Shuchō|qid=Q777413|lt=Shuchō|1=Shuchō}}), was first enshrined—gave the shrine its name"Atsuta Shrine Historical Records: Continued Compilation of Origins (Part I)": page 184. Furthermore, according to the "Owari Chronicle", since the main shrine enshrines five deities—{{ill|Amaterasu|en|Amaterasu|qid=Q455602|lt=Amaterasu Ōmikami|1=Amaterasu}}, {{ill|Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja|素盞嗚尊|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susanoo-no-Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スサノオ}}, {{ill|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|ja|日本武尊|1=Yamato Takeru|lt=Prince Yamato Takeru|3=ヤマトタケル|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamato Takeru|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to ヤマトタケル|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q461258}}, {{ill|Miyasu-hime no Mikoto|ja|宮簀媛命|lt=Miyasu-hime|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Miyasu-hime no Mikoto|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} and {{ill|Takeinadane no Mikoto|ja|建稲種命|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takeinadane no Mikoto|1=Takeinadane|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11486691}}—there is a theory that the name specifically refers to Amaterasu Omikami herself, the foremost of these deities {{#tag:ref|"From 'Owari Chronicle Atsuta': page 8 and 'Atsuta Shrine Record': "|group="Note"}}. It has also been written as "Ichinokami-mae" (一神前) {{#tag:ref|"From 'Atsuta Shrine Official Enshrinement Record'"|group="Note"}}, and as "Ichimusaki" (一御崎) {{#tag:ref|"From 'Atsuta Main and Subsidiary Shrine Divine Body Sacred Decree Collection'"page 171|group="Note"}}. ==References== ;Notes ;Sources {{reflist|3}} ==Bibliography== * "Kokushi Taikei, Volume 7" – Keizai Zasshisha, August 6, 1898 (Meiji 31) * Fukada Masakazu et al. – "Owari Chronicle Atsuta" – Hakubunsha, 1898 (Meiji 31) * Nagoya City Office – "Nagoya City History: Map" – Nagoya City Office, December 5, 1915 (Taishō 4) * Tsuda Masao – "Owari Province Place Name Studies" – Aichi Prefecture Kaibu-gun Educational Association, August 20, 1916 (Taishō 5) * Uematsu Yasushi – "Kazana Nihon Shoki, Volume 1" – Sakamoto Shinzō, June 28, 1920 (Taishō 9) * Atsuta Shrine Office – "Atsuta Shrine" – Atsuta Shrine Office, December 30, 1997 (Heisei 9) * Atsuta Shrine Office – "Atsuta Shrine Historical Records: Compilation of Origins" – Atsuta Shrine Office, March 17, 2002 (Heisei 14) * Atsuta Shrine Office – "Atsuta Shrine Historical Records: Continued Compilation of Origins (Part I)" – Atsuta Shrine Office, July 7, 2005 (Heisei 17) ==External Links== * [https://atsutajingu.or.jp/jingu/ Atsuta Shrine Homepage | Hatsu Ebisu, Shichigosan, Shrine Visit, Purification, Nagoya ｜ Atsuta Shrine] (Official Atsuta Shrine Website) {{Atsuta Faith}} {{Shinto Horizontal}} {{translated page|ja|一之御前神社|version=99985079|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:一之御前神社]] [[ja:南新宮社]] [[ja:高座結御子神社]] [[ja:氷上姉子神社]] [[ja:鈴之御前社]] {{wikidata link|Q55539277}} [[Category:Atsuta Shrine]] [[Category:Buildings and structures in Atsuta-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:History of Atsuta-ku, Nagoya]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Nagoya]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]]
